name: Marine War Risk Insurance
summary: Day 60 Monday. AWRP $10-14M/VOYAGE UNCHANGED — LAGGING INDICATOR; REPRICES ON OPERATIONAL CHANGE NOT PAPER BRENT. Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex / Monday Asia open — Branch 3 (positioning) defeats Branch 1 (structural break) and Branch 2 (theatrical band). War risk insurance Persian Gulf voyages: $10-14M per voyage UNCHANGED; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; Hull war 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x base; current 4-6x baseline (pre-crisis 0.25%). 329 vessels in Persian Gulf = ~$352B coverage gap that private markets are no longer providing. DFC $40B reinsurance partnership is policy backstop. Hormuz transit cost stack ~$6-10M per voyage all-in. Joint War Committee MAINTAINS entire Persian Gulf 'high-risk' zone designation — has not lifted on Branch 3 paper repricing. AWRP is LAGGING INDICATOR: reprices only on operational confirmation (Hormuz reopening, Iran kinetic cessation, formal IDF combat-readiness stand-down, Sadara JV resumption), NOT on Brent or Brent-implied probability. Yesterday's brief flagged 'Branch 1 outcome tests rate elevation toward 1.5-2.0% broad rate' — Branch 3 resolution makes this PATHWAY DORMANT. Conversely, Branch 3 paper Brent fade does NOT yet trigger insurance reduction — that requires sequential reinsurance chain rebuild ('cannot be compressed by political will' per Lloyd's). The marine-war-risk-insurance → brent-crude diagnostic edge confirms paper-physical decoupling: Brent gap-down -4.6%, AWRP holds. THIS IS THE CLEANEST DIAGNOSTIC OF Branch 3 BEING POSITIONING-DRIVEN, NOT OPERATIONAL.

current.cover_current: $10-14M per Persian Gulf voyage; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x; some easing to 0.35-0.45% on broader MEG routes. 329 vessels = ~$352B coverage gap.

signals.content: Insurance companies began cancelling war risk coverage for vessels in Gulf. Marine war risk insurance prices rising 50-100%. (Source: CNBC, The National)

War risk insurance at 1%+ hull value ($10-14M/voyage), 5x pre-crisis levels. Lloyd's JWC redesignated entire Arabian Gulf as conflict zone. Insurance premium IS the market's probability assessment — 'commercial risk logic has become an irregular warfare tool at Hormuz' per Irregular Warfare Journal. Hormuz tanker traffic collapsed 80%+. Commercial closure achieved through insurance costs making transit economically unviable, not through total military blockade. (Source: Lloyd's List, S&P Global, Irregular Warfare Journal)

WAR RISK INSURANCE UNCHANGED ON CEASEFIRE — Lloyd's: 'PHYSICAL NORMALISATION NOT COMPRESSED BY POLITICAL WILL.' Despite Brent crashing -13.5% on the ceasefire, hull war cover for Hormuz transit remains at $10-14M per voyage. Lloyd's: 'Even if a ceasefire takes hold, reinsurers must rebuild risk models from scratch. Each vessel seeking Gulf transit must be individually re-underwritten on a voyage-by-voyage basis. Pricing must clear across the entire reinsurance chain, from treaty reinsurer to P&I club to shipowner. Capacity that was withdrawn at the treaty level must be reinstated through negotiation, not announcement. This is a sequential, multi-party process that cannot be compressed by political will, executive order, or diplomatic breakthrough.' Implication: physical Hormuz flow restoration takes WEEKS even on a holding ceasefire. Brent's -13.5% move prices a faster restoration than is operationally possible.

Post-ceasefire: war risk insurance rates dipped to 0.35-0.45% of vessel value (from wartime peak 3.5-10%), but remain 7-10x pre-war levels. For a $150M LNG carrier, still ~$500K-$675K per voyage. LMA confirmed 88% of participants still have appetite. Neil Roberts (LMA): 'It is highly unlikely that trade into the Gulf will simply resume.' Reinsurers had exercised 72-hour cancellation clauses on clubs' charterers' liability at war start — restoring takes weeks, not days. Andrew James (Arthur J. Gallagher): underwriters 'already recognizing the ceasefire.' Gap: futures price ceasefire, insurance prices reality. Insurers are the best real-money probability assessment of ceasefire durability. (Source: TT News, CBS News, LMA, S&P Global)

Day 44. RATES STABLE + US REINSURANCE BACKSTOP DOUBLED TO $40B [CONFIRMED]. War risk insurance for Gulf transit remains at 0.35-0.45% of hull value — approximately where it settled after the initial ceasefire reaction on April 8-9. Still 40-80% above the pre-war 0.25% baseline. The material new development is that the US has doubled its reinsurance backstop capacity to $40B — a structural support that enables individual voyage underwriting to resume at the crisis-moderated levels even though zone designations remain. This is what makes the Maersk Red Sea transit (April 10-11) possible: not a lifted designation, but sufficient capacity at the treaty-reinsurance level for carrier-level war-risk lines to be re-underwritten case-by-case. Lloyd's JWC conflict-zone designation for Arabian Gulf is UNCHANGED — the trigger point is still 'watching.' For the investor: insurance is the sticky, slow-to-move signal. Insurance rates falling back to pre-war 0.25% baseline would be a stronger confirmation of ceasefire durability than any political announcement. Current 0.35-0.45% says insurers are pricing ~40-80% residual risk of resumption. That is consistent with the physical-futures oil gap ($24+) and with Neil Roberts's 'highly unlikely trade simply resumes' framing. The gap between futures pricing peace and insurance pricing residual risk remains the cleanest asymmetric signal in the market. (Sources: Lloyd's List, LMA, S&P Global, markets.md Section G, Insurance Journal)

EVENING CORRECTION: P&I clubs have NOT cancelled war risk cover (Lloyd's List). Coverage available but at prohibitive prices — $10-14M per VLCC transit, 2.5-5% hull value. LMA: 88% of Lloyd's war market has appetite to underwrite. The constraint is PRICE not AVAILABILITY. A diplomatic breakthrough could normalize premiums in weeks (insurers have capacity) vs months (if coverage had been actually suspended). De facto barrier for most commercial operators even though market exists. US DFC $40B political risk reinsurance facility attempting to fill gap. MORNING BRIEF CORRECTION: earlier signal stated clubs 'STOPPED offering' — Lloyd's List clarifies this was an overstatement; capacity remains but at commercially prohibitive levels that achieve the same de facto blockade through price deterrence rather than hard coverage withdrawal. The April 11 ceasefire-level estimate of 0.35-0.45% is now obsolete — the US naval blockade re-spiked premiums above pre-ceasefire levels. Kennedys Law: 'reshaped marine insurance risk landscape.' (Sources: Lloyd's List, CNBC, Kennedys Law, Caixin — April 14 evening)

War risk insurance EASING: 0.8-1.0% of hull value (down from 2.5% peak at blockade onset), still 4-6x pre-crisis (0.15-0.25%). LMA clarification: 'safety concerns, not insurance availability' driving reduced traffic — 88% of Lloyd's war market has appetite to underwrite at crisis premiums. Blockade paradoxically REDUCING random-attack risk (US Navy presence deters Iranian fast boats) while introducing more predictable interception risk. Market's probability assessment: ~5-8% probability of vessel loss/damage (down from ~15-20% at peak). US DFC $40B reinsurance facility attempting to fill gap. Kennedys Law: blockade 'reshaped marine insurance risk landscape.' Premium decline suggests market differentiating between blockade risk (predictable, manageable) and war risk (random, catastrophic). (Sources: Lloyd's List, LMA, WEF, IBTimes, Kennedys Law)

EVENING UPDATE: War risk insurance easing trajectory confirmed — from 5%+ hull value (war peak, ~$7.5M+ per VLCC voyage) to 2.5% (blockade onset) to current 0.8-1.0% (today). Still 4-6x pre-crisis (0.15-0.25%). LMA: 88% of Lloyd's war market still has underwriting appetite — 'safety not insurance' is the binding constraint on resumed Gulf traffic. Blockade paradoxically REDUCING random-attack risk (US Navy presence deters Iranian fast boats) while introducing predictable interception risk that insurers can model better. Market probability assessment: ~5-8% vessel loss/damage (from ~15-20% at peak). US DFC $40B reinsurance facility operational. Premium decline suggests market differentiating between blockade risk (predictable, insurable) and war risk (random, catastrophic). If diplomatic breakthrough occurs, premiums could normalize to 0.3-0.5% within weeks — insurers have capacity, just need safety assurance. (Sources: Lloyd's List, LMA, WEF, Kennedys Law)

GULF AWRP ~1% HULL — INSURERS DID NOT STEP BACK UP ON SATURDAY KINETIC YET; MONDAY RE-HIKE TO 1.2-1.8% LIKELY [CONFIRMED insurance baseline; REPORTED Monday direction]. Gulf AWRP remains ~1% hull post-ceasefire baseline (moderated from 2.5% blockade peak). Insurers stepped DOWN on April 8 ceasefire; have NOT stepped back up on Saturday April 18 IRGC kinetic (tanker + container + cruise-threat) as of Sunday 09:00 IST — the underwriting market is closed on weekends, so Monday is the first re-pricing window. Expected Monday AWRP re-hike: 1.2-1.8% hull. Significant KINETIC BROADENING consideration: Mein Schiff 4 master-logged IRGC VHF threat to 'fire at and destroy the ship' expands insurance scope from commercial-tanker to PASSENGER shipping. If a cruise/passenger vessel is actually hit (not the case as of Sunday 09:00), this triggers AUTOMATIC IMO-level emergency rather than purely commercial insurance response — fundamentally different capacity constraint since passenger shipping carries orders-of-magnitude higher liability exposure per vessel. Projectile (rocket/missile/drone) distinction from small-arms in container-ship incident also material: standoff munitions re-price differently than boarding/harassment because they expand hull-piercing casualty distribution. (Sources: Lloyd's List, Marsh, IBTimes Australia April 18, UKMTO advisory traffic)

AWRP NO SUNDAY REPUBLISH — MONDAY LONDON OPEN IS NEXT HARD TELL [REPORTED]. Lloyd's AWRP held ~1% hull through Friday; insurers did NOT step UP on Saturday's IRGC kinetic during weekend. Monday London open (07:00 IST) republish expected at 1.2-1.8%; 2%+ if cruise-class incident corroborates (Mein Schiff 4 master-report only as of Sunday). Araghchi Sunday X posts explicitly named the insurance market — not the IRGC kinetic — as the operational chokepoint mechanism: 'Ships hesitate because insurers fear the war of choice you initiated — not Iran.' Iran's civilian wing now sees insurance as the leverage point.

LLOYD'S AWRP ~1% HULL — NO SUNDAY REPUBLISH; MONDAY LONDON OPEN IS NEXT HARD TELL [REPORTED]. Lloyd's AWRP held ~1% hull through Friday despite Saturday IRGC kinetic — insurers did not step UP over the weekend. Evening brief expected Monday step to 1.5%+; morning-side data (Lloyd's List, Reinsurance News, LMA) suggests broader AWRP trend is MODERATION, with certain transits eased to 0.8-1.0% hull. Expected Monday move +20-80bps (milder than evening's +50-150bps). If Lloyd's republish prints above 1.5%, Goldman severe-tail ($115 Q4) moves to base; if holds at 1.0%, paper-traders pricing a step-up are disappointed. Crisis peak was 2.5% (60x pre-crisis 0.025-0.04%); current ~32-40x pre-war multiplier — elevated but easing.

USS SPRUANCE KINETIC ON COMMERCIAL-FLAGGED VESSEL — FIRST US-SIDE KINETIC-ON-IRANIAN-FLAG UNDER BLOCKADE [CONFIRMED ACTION]. USS Spruance disabled Iranian-flagged cargo vessel Touska in Gulf of Oman Sunday evening via Mk 45 naval-gun fire after 6 hours of failed VHF warnings. First use of live ordnance on an Iranian-flagged vessel in international waters by US under the blockade. Commercial-shipping threat surface now includes both IRGC-side kinetic (VLCC / container / cruise-ship targeting Saturday) AND US-side kinetic (Touska) — bi-directional risk. Insurance implications: AWRP models previously assumed Iran-side risk premium; now require US-side kinetic risk premium on Iranian-flagged commercial vessels. VLCC/Suezmax rate assessments remain elevated against 95% transit reduction.

AWRP HOLDING ~1.0% POST-EXTENSION; MODERATION THESIS VINDICATED [CONFIRMED]. Gulf AWRP ~1.0% certain transits; 0.8% with NCB residency; 2.5-5% US/UK/Israeli nexus. LMA March 23 statement 'safety concerns, not insurance availability, driving reduced vessel traffic' remains structural London market position. ≥1.5% step-up trigger requires kinetic event; extension removes imminent-kinetic risk. Thursday London republish expected 1.0-1.2% band (not ≥1.5%).

AWRP STEP-UP NOW EXPECTED THURSDAY LONDON REPUBLISH AT 1.5-2.0% BAND — MORNING 1.0-1.2% EXPECTATION INVALIDATED BY IRGC WEDNESDAY KINETIC [REPORTED — analyst expectation pending Thursday London open]. Wednesday's two confirmed kinetic incidents (firing on container ship + 8nm cargo ship) + IRGC formal seizure of MSC Francesca (Panama-flagged) + Epaminondas (Liberian-flagged, Greek-headquartered manager Technomar Shipping) re-prices the underwriting calculus structurally. The 'no radio warning' detail + multi-vessel commercial seizure inside the ceasefire envelope are operationally significant for Lloyd's JWC (the EU-flag-provocation reading earlier in the cycle is downgraded — both hulls fly flags of convenience, so underwriting impact is hull-value + carrier-exposure-driven, not flag-political). Brew's earlier framework — 'safety concerns, not insurance availability, driving reduced traffic' — holds form, but substance has migrated: kinetic has resumed, so safety concerns are now operationally validated rather than rhetorical-only. JWC has expanded high-risk zone to Bahrain/Djibouti/Kuwait/Oman/Qatar waters. Marine war risk for Gulf transit (US/UK/Israeli flag) at 3× standard quotes. Expect Friday spot AWRP quotes in 1.5-2.5% range and Brew's recovery probability rebases. Falsifiable: Thursday London republish at 1.5-2.0% = framework correct; republish stays at 1.0-1.2% = London market is mis-pricing Wednesday kinetic. THE MOST TIME-SENSITIVE TRIGGER IN THE GRAPH — falsification test within ~12 hours.

LLOYD'S AWRP THURSDAY LONDON REPUBLISH SPECIFIC NUMBER NOT YET IN OPEN-SOURCE ENGLISH PRESS [REPORTED — industry context consistent with 1.5-2.0% range]. Thursday morning English-language press (Lloyd's List, Seatrade Maritime, Insurance Business Mag) has not yet published a specific Thursday London republish number. Industry backdrop confirms: (a) London insurers launched $1B war risk consortium via Beazley mid-April; (b) rates 'renewable every seven days' at 1% of hull replacement value per S&P Global; (c) reported ranges 1-3% for Middle East routes; (d) Lloyd's List March: US/UK/Israeli-flagged ships charged 3x other flags. Absence of specific Thursday-morning step-up print is itself a datum — either republish has not yet been public-released, or step-up is being absorbed into existing 1-3% band rather than a discrete headline number. Wednesday evening brief's falsification window (~12 hours) effectively extended to Thursday European session close. Expect Lloyd's List subscriber-channel confirmation Thursday US morning ~17:30 IST.

PAPER-PHYSICAL GAP RE-OPENED — AWRP HOLDS 5-8x PRE-CRISIS WHILE PAPER BRENT FADES $9/BBL [CONFIRMED]. Thursday's paper-physical gap is the cleanest mechanism demonstration this crisis has produced. Paper Brent fell $9 ($106.15→$97.43) during US session as AWRP held 5-8x pre-crisis levels (1% of hull baseline; 5-10% for US/UK/Israeli flags; rates renewable every seven days per S&P Global). VLCC TD3C rates 'in uncharted territory' per Lloyd's List even on ceasefire days. Helium spot +40% vs pre-crisis. Natural gas +5.5% Thursday. Paper market oscillates on rhetoric; physical market prices operational continuation. The marine-war-risk-insurance → brent-crude edge is the diagnostic for regime identification — AWRP-paper divergence is the cleanest single-day signal of paper-physical structural divergence.

AWRP 2-6% HULL VALUE (8-24x PRE-CRISIS) — PAPER-PHYSICAL GAP AT WIDEST OF CRISIS; DFC $40B REINSURANCE FACILITY IS THE OPERATIONAL CAP [CONFIRMED]. Per S&P Global, Marketplace, Lloyd's List, IBTimes, Caixin: Gulf AWRP held 2-6% of ship value (from 0.25% pre-crisis = 8-24x multiplier). Lloyd's List: 'US/UK/Israeli-flagged vessels charged 3x others.' Joint War Committee maintains entire Persian Gulf as 'high-risk' zone. Mid-April reporting: 'cautious stabilization' on ceasefire-discussion reports, with some transits eased to 0.8-1% band. No fresh April 24 rate print in open press. Trump DFC $40B reinsurance facility partnership with US insurers is the CEASEFIRE-PRICING datum: government insurance backstopping commercial pullout. Signals both (a) commercial insurance market pullout is severe, (b) US government willing to underwrite continued Hormuz transit politically. Operational cap on AWRP escalation: above a threshold, DFC absorbs. Paper Brent -6.8% Friday cumulative $9-10/bbl two-session fade WHILE AWRP holds 8-24x pre-crisis unchanged = PAPER-PHYSICAL GAP WIDEST OF CRISIS. Cleanest mechanism demonstration to date of marine-war-risk-insurance → brent-crude diagnostic edge per markets.md F2 — second consecutive day the mechanism is operationally demonstrated. This edge is now the cleanest regime-identification tool in the graph.
signals.verification: CONFIRMED industry physical indicators; no April 24 specific rate print in open press.

signals.content: AWRP $10-14M PER PERSIAN GULF VOYAGE; 329 VESSELS = ~$352B COVERAGE GAP; DFC $40B BACKSTOP [CONFIRMED]. War risk insurance Persian Gulf voyages: $10-14M per voyage; broad rates 0.8-1.5%; US/UK/Israeli flags 3x base. Some easing to 0.35-0.45% on broader Middle East Gulf routes. WEF data: 329 vessels operating in Persian Gulf = ~$352B in insurance coverage that private markets are no longer providing — this is the magnitude of the physical-disruption gap, materially larger than any prior crisis. DFC $40B reinsurance partnership remains the policy backstop and operational ceiling. Joint War Committee maintains entire Persian Gulf as 'high-risk' zone. CRITICAL FRAMEWORK REFINEMENT: Yesterday's brief encoded paper-physical gap as 'widest of crisis' on Brent -6.8% data; Saturday data audit reveals Friday Brent actually closed ~$105 (not $97.94). Both paper and physical are now confirmed to be in crisis-pricing mode — paper-physical gap is materially NARROWER than yesterday's framework implied. The marine-war-risk-insurance edge to brent-crude remains a regime-identification tool but its 'cleanest' status (per yesterday) is downgraded — paper has converged with physical at $103-107 band. (Sources: Lloyd's List, WEF, S&P Global, Joint War Committee)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED industry data; CRITICAL FRAMEWORK REFINEMENT post-Brent-correction.

signals.content: TRUMP CANCELLATION DIRECTLY IMPACTS INSURER PRICING — NEW DIRECT EDGE PROPOSED [PENDING BRANCH 1 ACTIVATION]. Per Senior Market F1: Trump's Saturday cancellation of the Witkoff-Kushner Islamabad dispatch directly impacts insurer pricing of Persian Gulf voyages via diplomatic-track-breakdown signal. Currently routed through the iran-irgc-shipping cascade; the new direct edge shortens the chain by one hop. Activation criterion: Branch 1 Monday outcome (Sunday Globex Brent above $108) confirms structural-break read; AWRP would test rate elevation toward 1.5-2.0% broad rate. Insurance restoration requires sequential reinsurance chain rebuild ('cannot be compressed by political will' per Lloyd's List April 8) so even Branch 3 (below $103) outcomes produce delayed insurer-perceived-risk reduction. Cost-stack on a single Hormuz transit reportedly $6-10M per voyage all-in (AWRP + IRGC toll + crew/freight surcharges per Lloyd's List, hormuztoll.com April 23). DFC $40B reinsurance partnership remains operational ceiling. (Sources: Senior Market F1 dossier; Lloyd's List; hormuztoll.com)

AWRP $10-14M/VOYAGE UNCHANGED ON BRANCH 3 BRENT GAP — LAGGING-INDICATOR DIAGNOSTIC EMPIRICALLY VINDICATED [CONFIRMED]. Brent gapped -4.6% to $100.47 on Sunday Globex / Monday Asia open — Branch 3 (positioning) defeats Branch 1 (structural break) and Branch 2 (theatrical band). War risk insurance UNCHANGED at $10-14M per Persian Gulf voyage; Hull war 0.8-1.5%; current 4-6x pre-crisis baseline (0.25%). Joint War Committee MAINTAINS entire Persian Gulf 'high-risk' zone designation — has not lifted on Branch 3 paper repricing. Yesterday's brief flagged 'Branch 1 outcome tests rate elevation toward 1.5-2.0% broad rate; Branch 3 sees insurer-perceived-risk reduction with delay' — Branch 3 hit the tape but AWRP did not move. Mechanism: AWRP is LAGGING INDICATOR; reprices only on operational confirmation (Hormuz reopening, Iran kinetic cessation, formal IDF combat-readiness stand-down, Sadara JV resumption — none of which has happened), NOT on Brent or Brent-implied probability. Insurance restoration requires sequential reinsurance chain rebuild ('cannot be compressed by political will' per Lloyd's List). The marine-war-risk-insurance → brent-crude diagnostic edge: Brent gap-down -4.6%, AWRP holds = paper-physical DECOUPLING demonstrated. THIS IS THE CLEANEST DIAGNOSTIC OF BRANCH 3 BEING POSITIONING-DRIVEN, NOT OPERATIONAL — Branch 3 is a re-rating of TIME-EXTENSION risk in paper Brent (8-16 weeks → 4-8 weeks), not LEVEL change in physical operational stress (Hormuz still functionally closed; Sadara still shut; war-risk insurance still 4-6x pre-crisis). For investor: AWRP holding $10-14M is the empirical refutation of any 'crisis is over' read on the paper Brent gap. The crisis architecture is intact at the operational layer; only the time-extension paper-pricing is compressing. Branch 3 + AWRP unchanged = clean signal that paper Brent has converged into a 4-8 week resolution path under Russia-mediated architecture. (Sources: Lloyd's List; WEF; S&P Global; Joint War Committee; Senior Market dossier C5 Branch 3)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED — AWRP unchanged on Branch 3 Brent gap; lagging-indicator diagnostic vindicated.

edges.context: Day 56 Thursday. Lloyd's AWRP Thursday London republish: specific number not yet in open press. Industry backdrop: Beazley $1B war risk consortium mid-April; rates 'renewable every seven days' at 1% hull replacement value per S&P Global; reported ranges 1-3% for Middle East routes. Either republish has not yet been public-released, or step-up is being absorbed into existing 1-3% band rather than discrete headline.

Day 56 Thursday. AWRP holds 5-8x pre-crisis. Frontline -2.0% Thursday on ceasefire-absorption mean reversion + Brent fade. VLCC TD3C 'in uncharted territory' per Lloyd's List even on ceasefire days. War-risk premium embedded in spot rates.

Day 60 Monday. CLEANEST DIAGNOSTIC OF BRANCH 3 BEING POSITIONING-DRIVEN, NOT OPERATIONAL: Brent gap -4.6% to $100.47, AWRP holds $10-14M/voyage UNCHANGED (4-6x pre-crisis). Paper-physical decoupling empirically demonstrated — Branch 3 is re-rating of TIME-EXTENSION risk (8-16 weeks → 4-8 weeks), not LEVEL change in physical operational stress. Edge weight upgraded back to 9.0 on Branch-3-vindicated diagnostic role.

Day 60 Monday. Branch 3 resolution → trump-cancellation-as-diplomatic-breakdown-signal reframed: Trump cancellation impacted Sat-Sun-Mon pre-Globex sentiment but Branch 3 paper Brent gap compressed Hormuz risk premium. DFC $40B reinsurance facility remains operational ceiling. Branch 1 activation pathway DORMANT until fresh structural-break catalyst.

trigger_points.condition: Lloyd's JWC removes Arabian Gulf from conflict zone designation
trigger_points.mechanism: Insurance restoration is the binding constraint on physical Hormuz reopening. Until Lloyd's JWC formally lifts the conflict-zone designation, individual voyage underwriting persists at extreme rates. This is a more operationally meaningful trigger than the political ceasefire — the insurance market signals when normal commerce can actually resume.

trigger_points.condition: Lloyd's AWRP Thursday April 23 London republish prints at 1.5-2.0% step-up band (vs 1.0-1.2% morning expectation)
trigger_points.mechanism: Most time-sensitive trigger in graph — falsification test within ~12 hours of trigger creation. IRGC Wednesday kinetic (container ship firing + MSC Francesca/Epaminondas seizure) re-prices underwriting calculus. 1.5-2.0% republish = framework correct, structural re-rate of Gulf war risk; pushes Goldman severe-tail $115 Q4 toward base-case probability. 1.0-1.2% republish = London market is mis-pricing Wednesday kinetic, AWRP step-up thesis falsified, paper-physical convergence delayed. Friday spot quotes 1.5-2.5% expected. Falsifiable: Thursday London republish at 11:00 GMT. | RESOLVED 2026-04-24: No Thursday or Friday specific rate print surfaced in open English press. Industry backdrop consistent with 2-6% band for Middle East routes per S&P Global / Lloyd's List / Caixin. Step-up is being absorbed into 1-3% band rather than discrete headline. Trigger closes as NEITHER framework confirmed NOR falsified — the republish simply did not surface. AWRP at 2-6% is elevated but within the pre-established band.
